text.title: Dev Methodologies — Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the dev-methodologies-architecture methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every foreign key column has an index. Verify with a migration audit.
rule.rationale: Un-indexed FKs cause N×M table-scans on JOINs; the largest single source of slow queries.

rule.statement: ORM queries that follow FK chains use select_related (or .include / .with relations). Avoid N+1 by default.
rule.rationale: ORMs lazy-load by default; following relations naively turns 1 query into N.

rule.statement: Every cache key has an explicit TTL. Indefinite TTLs are forbidden in shared cache.
rule.rationale: Cache invalidation is hard; explicit TTL guarantees eventual freshness even without invalidation.

rule.statement: Every background job is idempotent: retrying it must not double-charge, double-send, or double-create.
rule.rationale: Job runners retry on transient failure; non-idempotent jobs corrupt state on retry.

rule.statement: API responses use a consistent envelope: {data, meta, errors}. errors[] is reserved for unexpected failures.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent envelopes force every client to handle N response shapes; one envelope handles all cases.

rule.statement: All application logs are structured JSON with request_id, user_id, event, level, fields. No printf-style logs in production code.
rule.rationale: Structured logs are queryable; printf logs require regex-grep at 3am.

rule.statement: If the service is frontend-only, batch-only, or has no tests, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Backend patterns require a test net; pure frontend is out of scope.
